questions.question: Explain First Fit, Best Fit, and Worst Fit allocation strategies with examples. Which is generally preferred and why?
keyPoints: First Fit: First hole that fits, fast
Best Fit: Smallest fitting hole, minimal waste
Worst Fit: Largest hole, quick fragmentation
First Fit preferred: Fast and good performance

questions.question: Given memory partitions of 100KB, 500KB, 200KB, 300KB, 600KB (in order) and processes requiring 212KB, 417KB, 112KB, 426KB, show allocation using First Fit and Best Fit.
keyPoints: Draw memory state after each allocation

questions.question: Differentiate between internal and external fragmentation. How does paging solve external fragmentation?
keyPoints: Paging: Fixed-size pages eliminate external fragmentation
But introduces internal fragmentation in last page
